title: Should I even bother trying to collect the manga volumes now
display_title: Should I even bother trying to collect the manga volumes now
wikitext:
[]: Should I even bother trying to collect the manga volumes now             Quote Post by StormSky92 » Sat May 18, 2013 12:25 am Hello. I'm new here, and I joined pretty much just to ask this question. I know i'm a little late to the party, but i've only recently gotten into Evangelion. I have been looking around online, and it seems like volumes 9 and 10 of the manga are impossible to find. It's very frustrating. I've searched for the other threads on here about it, but they are all older, so maybe some new way of obtaining these volumes came to light and I missed it entirely. I know about the 3-in-1 volumes released by VIZ, however my problem with them is that they look too big. I really don't like the big, bulky 3-in-1 volumes, so I was hoping it would be possible to get the individual volumes. I read some people claiming the Chuang Yi releases were "great" or something like that, but I have problems with their website loading pages. Basically, should I just give in and buy the 3-in-1s, or there is some hope that volumes 9 and 10 are still out there somewhere? (Books A Million has both listed at $5.99 and says they are supposedly available to ship within 7 days, but I don't believe that)

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat May 18, 2013 1:31 am They are out there, but you need to look around for a while to find them. Obviously there are people getting the larger volumes, and this is freeing up some copies of the individual volumes.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sat May 18, 2013 6:50 am The Viz 3-in-1s are very good, and I actually prefer that size - But to each their own. Chuang Yi volumes are alright as well (Madman joint distribute them in Australia). I'd actually say I prefer them to the single Viz tankōbon releases. Better print quality, paper and they have dust jackets, etcetera. I can vouch for both.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat May 18, 2013 7:19 am Now is actually a pretty good time to get into the manga, thanks to the omnibus/3-in-1 release that Viz is doing. I imagine that they'll manage to release the whole rest of the series in this format, including the final volume at some point this year. I did a post about it with a bit of info: http://www.evamonkey.com/the-evangelion-omnibus-is-a-real-thing/

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Sat May 18, 2013 12:36 pm Ya just get the omnibus's, thats what I've been doing. Really easy way to get the whole series as they come out.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sat May 18, 2013 12:59 pm I found all the tankobon released so far in a little over a year. It's not too hard.

[]:             Quote Post by StormSky92 » Sat May 18, 2013 3:07 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Now is actually a pretty good time to get into the manga, thanks to the omnibus/3-in-1 release that Viz is doing. I imagine that they'll manage to release the whole rest of the series in this format, including the final volume at some point this year. I did a post about it with a bit of info: http://www.evamonkey.com/the-evangelion-omnibus-is-a-real-thing/

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat May 18, 2013 3:17 pm StormSky92 wrote: About your post, when it says "Clean versions of the actual covers of volumes 1-3 are printed in the front of the collection." so, it does have the covers of the original volumes 1-3 in it? Have the other omnibus releases done that as well?

[]:             Quote Post by StormSky92 » Sat May 18, 2013 4:01 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Yup. So it has full page artwork of the original covers inside, and they have no text or logos so you can really appreciate the artwork.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat May 18, 2013 5:06 pm I've not noticed that in the three volumes out so far. The paper is thinner than in the individual volumes, but smoother and much whiter, having a better quality feel to it, and it seems just as opaque.

[]:             Quote Post by Paranoid » Sat May 18, 2013 5:21 pm StormSky92 wrote: Interesting. That's definitely a major point in favor of the omnibus. However, i've read reviews online that say the ink bleeds through the page occasionally, and it can be really distracting. Is it really that bad, or it being blow out of proportion?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat May 18, 2013 5:44 pm Paranoid wrote: it includes all of the original art and extras, so you don't lose anything.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary Cloud » Sat May 18, 2013 6:31 pm I've been lurking for awhile, and was hoping a thread like this would pop up. I joined so I could just be straightforward and ask: Which one does everything think, overall, is "better" to try and get if you have the choice between the individual volumes and the omnibuses?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat May 18, 2013 9:12 pm Legendary Cloud wrote: I've been lurking for awhile, and was hoping a thread like this would pop up. I joined so I could just be straightforward and ask: Which one does everything think, overall, is "better" to try and get if you have the choice between the individual volumes and the omnibuses?

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sun May 19, 2013 12:59 am 3 for 1 is hard to beat, if I were to get into the manga now without ever having picked up a volume I would opt for that.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sun May 19, 2013 5:11 am StormSky92 wrote: Where can you get the Chuang Yi volumes from? I looked online briefly, but I couldn't find anything.

[]:             Quote Post by StormSky92 » Sun May 19, 2013 3:33 pm Well, I have been thoroughly persuaded to just get the Omnibuses. I saw a video on Youtube, and those color pages look awesome. The extras looks like they are formatted better, and the white pages really make the art stand out. It just sucks that I have to wait until August for the fourth omnibus, but oh well. I doubt they'll make a set of 13 & 14, so i'll probably have to buy those individually, but oh well. It will a lot easier on my shelf space (and my wallet).

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun May 19, 2013 4:04 pm StormSky92 wrote: I doubt they'll make a set of 13 & 14

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun May 19, 2013 4:13 pm More to the point, it would be beyond stupid not to complete the set in the matching size and quality. I'm hoping for some extra essays or something (or at least the ones missing from the third volume!) to help bulk out the final volume, but I don't really expect anything much new.

[]: Evangelion 3-in-1 (Omnibus) question             Quote Post by delispin25 » Tue May 21, 2013 7:07 pm I never got to read the Eva manga while it was published one volume at a time, so of course I was really excited to hear about the Omnibus that Viz was going to release. So far I've bought every volume. While reading, a question arose, if they are serializing the manga as a 3-in-1, what's going to happen with the last 2 volumes. Will they release the last two volumes in a 2-in-1, or will they not release them at all, since they are already out. Maybe they will put in all the Angel Kiss that they left out, along with other supplements, it would suck if they didn't.
